world.title: Bop Foundations
world.description: A slower, beginner-first path through Bop fundamentals with extra practice before each new concept.
world.story_intro: Take your time, practice each idea, and build confidence before moving on.

levels.title: Step by Step
levels.description: Move to the goal using simple right moves.
levels.tutorial:
# Move Basics
Each move command takes one step. Repeat the same command until you reach the goal.

levels.title: Longer Walk
levels.description: Reach a farther goal with clean, consistent movement.
levels.hint: Keep moving right until the bot stands on the goal tile.
levels.tutorial:
# Practice Round
This is the same idea as level 1, just longer. Build fluency with movement.

levels.title: Turn the Corner
levels.description: Use a mix of directions to reach the goal.
levels.hint: Move right first, then down to the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Four Directions
Bop movement is not just right. Combine directions to route around walls.

levels.title: Movement Checkpoint
levels.description: Navigate a short route with walls and one final turn.
levels.hint: Find the gap in the wall row, then route to the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Checkpoint
You have enough movement tools now. Solve this route before adding new commands.

levels.title: First Gem Pickup
levels.description: Collect the gem and then reach the goal.
levels.hint: Stand on the gem tile before calling grab().
levels.tutorial:
# Collecting Gems
Use grab() only when your bot is standing on a gem tile.

levels.title: Two Gems in a Row
levels.description: Collect both gems, then finish at the goal.
levels.hint: Move and grab in sequence so no gem is missed.
levels.tutorial:
# Sequencing
Your code runs top to bottom. Plan the full path before you type.

levels.title: Gem Practice Checkpoint
levels.description: A longer line with empty spaces between gems.
levels.hint: Do not call grab() on empty tiles.
levels.tutorial:
# Checkpoint
Practice movement plus gem collection before introducing loops.

levels.title: Your First repeat Loop
levels.description: Use repeat to avoid writing the same line many times.
levels.hint: Increase the repeat count until the bot reaches the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# repeat
Use repeat N { ... } when you know exactly how many times an action should run.

levels.title: repeat and grab
levels.description: Apply a repeating move pattern while collecting gems.
levels.hint: Use a 2-step loop body: move right, grab if gem_here(), move right. Repeat that pattern 4 times.
levels.tutorial:
# Loop Patterns
A good loop body matches the shape of the level.

levels.title: Repeat Checkpoint
levels.description: Use loops and path planning to collect gems in a small route.
levels.hint: Go right to the center opening, drop down to the gem row, clear left gem first, then sweep right through the remaining gems to the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Checkpoint
This level combines movement, grabbing, and looping in one puzzle.

levels.title: Only Grab When Needed
levels.description: Use if gem_here() so grab() only runs on gem tiles.
levels.hint: Conditionals are useful when not every tile needs the same action.
levels.tutorial:
# if Conditions
if gem_here() lets you write safe code that works on mixed tiles.

levels.title: if Practice Lap
levels.description: Collect all gems in a small map with branches.
levels.hint: Move right in a loop, but only grab when there is a gem on the current tile.
levels.tutorial:
# Conditional Practice
Now use if in a loop where tiles are less predictable.

levels.title: while Until the Wall
levels.description: Use while path_ahead() to move until blocked.
levels.hint: Use move("forward") inside the while loop to keep moving right until you hit the wall, then turn right and repeat.
levels.tutorial:
# while Loops
Use while when you do not want to hardcode an exact count.

levels.title: while + if Together
levels.description: Combine a loop and a conditional to collect gems on the way.
levels.hint: Move first, then check for a gem on the current tile.
levels.tutorial:
# Combined Control Flow
This is a core Bop pattern: loop forward, conditionally act.

levels.title: Checkpoint: Gentle Maze
levels.description: A calm maze that reinforces planning, loops, and safe grabs.
levels.hint: Take the center opening to get the left gem first, return to the top lane, move to the right opening for the second gem, then climb back to the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Checkpoint
You are now combining movement, conditionals, and route planning confidently.

levels.title: Bop Foundations Finale
levels.description: Final beginner challenge: collect gems and finish with clean structure.
levels.hint: Section it: grab the left gem, route through the middle bridge to the top-right lane, drop to the bottom-right gem, then return up and finish at the goal.
levels.tutorial:
# Foundations Complete
This level is intentionally broader. Solve it with the patterns you practiced.
